title: ds-viz-scatter - option matrix (light)
description: Light-theme companion. Test bench for splunk.scatter. Verifies all options from the 10.4 PDF: x/y/category DOS bindings, markerSize, seriesColors, seriesColorsByField, axis abbreviations/visibility/scale (linear|log), gridlines, axis title visibility, legend, backgroundColor, resultLimit. Two-series and three-series patterns covered.

ds_xy_simple.name: two-series xy - response_time vs requests, no category

options.query: | makeresults count=30 | streamstats count AS rn | eval requests = round(50 + random()%450) | eval response_time = round(50 + (requests/5) + (random()%80)) | table requests response_time

ds_xy_categorized.name: three-series xy with category - latency vs throughput per service

options.query: | makeresults count=60 | streamstats count AS rn | eval service = case(rn%3==0,"api", rn%3==1,"db", 1==1,"cache") | eval throughput = case(service=="api",100+random()%400, service=="db",50+random()%200, 1==1,500+random()%1500) | eval latency = case(service=="api",20+random()%80, service=="db",100+random()%300, 1==1,5+random()%20) | table throughput latency service

ds_status_by_hour.name: status x date_hour - PDF canonical example shape

options.query: | makeresults count=72 | streamstats count AS rn | eval date_hour = (rn-1)%24 | eval status = case(rn%4==0,"200", rn%4==1,"301", rn%4==2,"404", 1==1,"500") | eval count = case(status=="200",100+random()%400, status=="301",10+random()%30, status=="404",5+random()%50, 1==1,1+random()%10) | stats sum(count) AS count by status, date_hour | table date_hour count status

ds_log_scale.name: wide-range data - candidates for log scale on y

options.query: | makeresults count=40 | streamstats count AS rn | eval x_val = rn | eval y_val = pow(2, rn%12) + random()%50 | table x_val y_val

options.markdown:
# `splunk.scatter` - option matrix (light)

Scatter charts show relationships between two numeric dimensions, optionally split by a third categorical field. Three DOS bindings drive the chart: `x`, `y`, and `category`.

Verified options from the **10.4 PDF**:
- DOS bindings: `x`, `y`, `category` (default to `seriesByIndex(0|1|2)`)
- Field labels: `xField`, `yField`, `categoryField` (defaults via `> x | getField()`)
- `markerSize` (number, default 4), `resultLimit` (default 50000)
- Color: `seriesColors`, `seriesColorsByField`, `backgroundColor`
- Axes: `xAxis*` and `yAxis*` (Min/Max, Scale linear|log, Abbreviation, Title/Label/Line/Tick visibility, MajorTickInterval, ExtendedRange, WithZero, MinorGridLines, RoundedXAxisLabels, LabelRotation)
- Legend: `legendDisplay`, `legendTruncation`

viz_default.title: 1. Default - two columns (x, y)
viz_default.description: No options. Defaults to seriesByIndex(0) and seriesByIndex(1).

viz_categorized.title: 3. Three-series with category
viz_categorized.description: x=throughput, y=latency, category=service. Each service gets its own color.

viz_marker_big.title: 4. Large markers (markerSize 10)
viz_marker_big.description: Default markerSize is 4. Bigger markers help when point count is low.

viz_custom_palette.title: 5. Custom seriesColors
viz_custom_palette.description: Brand palette overrides default. Maps to series in alphabetical order.

viz_colors_by_field.title: 6. seriesColorsByField - lock service to color
viz_colors_by_field.description: Pin a specific color to a category value regardless of series order.

viz_log_y.title: 7. Log scale on y
viz_log_y.description: yAxisScale=log. Useful when y spans several orders of magnitude.

viz_axis_extras.title: 8. Axis tuning - min/max, abbreviation, extended range
viz_axis_extras.description: yAxisMin=0, yAxisAbbreviation=auto, showXAxisExtendedRange=false. Tightens the canvas to the data.

viz_axis_labels_hidden.title: 9. Hide axis titles, keep labels
viz_axis_labels_hidden.description: Titles off (xAxisTitleVisibility=hide), labels still on. Cleaner for grids of small charts.

viz_no_axes_at_all.title: 10. Stripped - no titles, no legend, no minor grid
viz_no_axes_at_all.description: Editorial / KPI-tile style scatter. Show only the points.

viz_canonical_pdf.title: 12. Canonical PDF example - status x date_hour
viz_canonical_pdf.description: Reproduces the splunkd_access example from the 10.4 PDF: x=date_hour, y=count, category=status.

viz_tinted.title: 13. backgroundColor tint
viz_tinted.description: Custom panel background. Pairs with brand palette.
